headline: Week five: back to work with a surplus, calf still quiet
narrative: Back to full load after the deload, and the fresh legs did what fresh legs should: vert landed a small surplus over target, with time on feet just past the ask too. The focus was steeper climbing with a downhill eccentric emphasis, and the centrepiece delivered exactly that — a big Kinder round over Swine's Back, Kinder Low, Kinder Scout and Grindslow Knoll, the biggest single-day vert of the block, with the descending that focus demands. Around it sat a club run, a fine Higher Moor morning, and Sunday hike reps on Mellor Moor. Saturday's parkrun was taken as a walk — the sensible call on a loaded week.

The calf: not a murmur, five weeks running now. That silence, plus a week that hit its shape as well as its number, keeps the build roughly where the plan wants it heading into the meat of Base. Week six raises the target again and asks for one thing above all — a long mountain day. Protect the rest of the week around it and make that day count.
